t_ch: 这样，大家就不会被娜塔莎姐姐骂啦。谢谢{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，你对阿丽娜最好了~
t_en: This way, no one will get in trouble with Natasha. Thank you, you are the best!

t_ch: 你问这些纸团怎么办？嘻嘻，交给阿丽娜吧，大家的心意…当然是要好好珍藏呀。
t_en: You mean how am I going to deal with all these paper balls? Hehe, just leave them to me... Of course I'm going to treasure their wishes.

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}找到其它纸团了吗？快给阿丽娜看看吧。
t_en: So, you've found all the other paper balls? Please show them to me.

t_ch: 啊，外来者，是你们啊。来的正好，我有个差事想拜托你们，事成之后必有重谢。
t_en: Ah, outsiders. You've come just in time. I have something to ask of you. You will be rewarded, of course.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我想让你们到恶魔之胃里搜集一些古代遗物。
t_en: To tell you the truth, I want you to go to the Devil's Stomach and collect some ancient relics.

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我记得你不再鉴定古代遗物了。
t_en: I thought you weren't in the appraisal business anymore.

t_ch: 谁说要鉴定了？我只是让你去找，没说别的——你可别到处胡说八道啊！
t_en: Who said anything about appraisals? I just want you to go for a search — don't go spewing nonsense!

t_ch: 大致的地点我已经标出来了。拿到之后直接回我这儿来，别告诉任何人！
t_en: I've marked their rough location. Bring them back to me once you've found them. Don't tell anyone!

t_ch: 按照巴列维的指示果然找到了古代遗物，他甚至没有来过这里，他是怎么知道的呢？
t_en: You found the ancient relics that Balaway was looking for. Without having been here, how did he know where to look?

t_ch: 好家伙，手脚够利索啊。东西给我，钱你拿走。
t_en: Oh my, you're just as reliable as you look. Give them to me. Take your reward and leave.

t_ch: 多问一句…
t_en: Can I ask...

t_ch: 我懂我懂，你想问的是，我怎么知道恶魔之胃里藏着好东西，是吧？
t_en: I know what you want to ask. How did I know there's some good stuff in the Devil's Stomach, right?

t_ch: 那我就直说了，这都是客人透露给我的。我只是用了点小手段，撬开了他们的嘴巴…嘿嘿。
t_en: Well, I'll just tell you. One of my customers tipped me off. It took me some time, but with a few tricks, he opened up to me... Hehe.

t_ch: 哎哟，我跟你说这么多干嘛？行了行了，任务结了，报酬也领了——要是没有别的事，各位就请回吧！
t_en: Wait, why am I telling you so much? The mission is over and you have your reward. If there's nothing else, please leave!

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助。
t_en: I hear you need help.

t_ch: 我找到了这些东西。
t_en: I found these.

t_ch: 喂，{NICKNAME}！快过来——安静点，别发出声音！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! Come over here — don't make any sound!

t_ch: 又要玩捉迷藏吗？
t_en: Are we playing hide-and-seek again?

t_ch: 什么捉迷藏啊，这次咱们要干的事可比捉迷藏有趣多了！
t_en: Nah, this time we are going to do something much more fun than hide-and-seek!

t_ch: 你这孩子，怎么总鬼鬼祟祟的…
t_en: How come you are always sneaking around...

t_ch: 什么叫鬼鬼祟祟！我可是负责给鼹鼠党出谋划策的干部，隐秘行动就是我的工作！
t_en: What do you mean by sneaking around! I'm the cadre in charge of making plans for The Moles. Covert operations are what I do.

t_ch: 告诉你吧，今天我们要执行一项绝密任务！你作为鼹鼠党的「荣誉队员」，现在被我征用了！
t_en: Let me tell you, today we have a top-secret mission! As an honorary member of The Moles, you are now being commandeered by me!

t_ch: 我有拒绝的权利吗？
t_en: Can I say no?

t_ch: 那还用说嘛？当然没有！
t_en: Of course not!

t_ch: 全听你的，老大。
t_en: It's all up to you, boss.

t_ch: 什、什么老大呀，别瞎说！要是被虎克老大听到就惨啦。
t_en: Wh—what boss? Don't talk nonsense! I will be in trouble if Boss Hook heard it.

t_ch: 别扯闲天啦，时间不等人，万一把任务搞砸了可就没法交差咯。
t_en: Enough chitchat. We're losing the daylight. We can't mess this up.

t_ch: 我们出发吧，目的地——「地火」的秘密集会所！
t_en: Let's go! To the secret gathering place of Wildfire!

t_ch: 怎、怎么办呢…这里有两箱应急药品，我到、到底该先送哪一边？
t_en: Wh—What should I do... There are two crates of emergency medicine here. Which box should I deliver first?

t_ch: 不、不管了！就先送这一箱吧，另、另外一箱等我回来再说……
t_en: N—Never mind! I'm gonna deliver this box first, and then, the other one after I come back...

t_ch: 快，荣誉队员！「地火」的家伙走了，我们快点上去看看！
t_en: Quick, honorary member! The guy from Wildfire left, let's go have a look!

t_ch: 秘密任务就是偷人家的东西？
t_en: So the secret mission is to steal from others?

t_ch: 什、什么啊，你别胡说八道！鼹鼠党才不会干那种事！
t_en: N—Nonsense! The Moles wouldn't do that!

t_ch: 先告诉我你的目的…
t_en: Tell me what you're up to first...

t_ch: 哎呀，你非要在这种关头问问题嘛！
t_en: Ugh, do you have to ask questions right now?

t_ch: 我就告诉你吧，鼹鼠党要抢在「地火」发现之前，把那箱药物送到需要的人手上。
t_en: Here's the deal. The Moles is going to deliver the medicine to those in need before Wildfire finds out.

t_ch: 至于理由…哎，现在不是解释的时候！总之先快行动起来吧。
t_en: As for the reason... well, now is not the time to explain! We should hurry.

t_ch: 走，咱们去看看那箱东西！
t_en: Come on, let's take a look at that box!

t_ch: 看，箱子上面写了配送地址！{NICKNAME}，我们快去给有需要的人送药吧。
t_en: Look, the shipping address is written on the box! {NICKNAME}, let's send it to those in need now.

t_ch: 一定要赶在「地火」反应过来之前把东西送到，嘿嘿！
t_en: We have to deliver it before the Wildfire realizes, hee-hee!

t_ch: 你…你们是？这打扮看着不像「地火」的人呐……
t_en: Who are you? You don't dress like a Wildfire member...

t_ch: 奶奶，我们是「鼹鼠党」的派送员！以后需要帮忙的话，记得联系我们哦！
t_en: Lady, we are couriers from The Moles! If you need help in the future, remember to call us!

t_ch: …「鼹鼠党」？那不是一帮小孩子吗……
t_en: ..."The Moles"? Isn't that a bunch of kids...

t_ch: 哦…呵呵，我明白啦。谢谢你们，我等这件快递已经等了小半天了。
t_en: Oh... hehe, I get it. Thank you, I have been waiting for this package all day.

t_ch: 我先回去了，家里那位老头还指着这箱药呢。希望你们能继续加油干，「鼹鼠党」的年轻人……
t_en: I should go home now. My husband is waiting for this box of medicine. Keep up the good work, you kids from The Moles...

t_ch: 太棒了，荣誉队员！这次秘密行动执行得非常完美，值得褒奖！
t_en: Great job, honorary member! This secret mission was executed perfectly! You deserve some kudos!

t_ch: 我觉得「地火」也能处理这事…
t_en: I think Wildfire can handle this too...

t_ch: 我还是没太明白…
t_en: I still don't quite get it...

t_ch: 唔，大人的脑子可真笨呀，你到现在还没明白嘛？
t_en: Well, grown-ups are so silly. You still don't get it?

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你，这都是「鼹鼠党振兴计划」的第一步哦——找到「地火」工作中的疏漏，然后靠我们敏捷的行动力把空缺补上！
t_en: It's a secret, but I'll tell you. This is the first step of The Moles Reinvigoration Plan — find the work that Wildfire has neglected and take it up right away!

t_ch: 只要我们坚持这么做，鼹鼠党很快就能在下层区积攒名声啦…说不定有一天还能取代「地火」，成为最受欢迎的超级组织！
t_en: If we keep doing this, The Moles will soon build up a reputation in the Underworld... Maybe one day it will replace Wildfire and become the most popular super organization!

t_ch: 那就祝你们顺利…
t_en: Good luck to you then...

t_ch: 哦哦，燃起来了！
t_en: Ooh, that's a great ambition!

t_ch: 总而言之，这次你干得相当不错，值得表扬！我会把你的事迹汇报给虎克大人的，放心吧。
t_en: You did a pretty good job this time and deserve some credit! I will tell Boss Hook about it. Don't worry.

t_ch: 下次我有事召唤你的话，你也要随叫随到哦——那就这样吧，鼹鼠党，解散！
t_en: Next time I call on you for something, don't forget to show up right away — so that's it, The Moles, dismiss!

t_ch: 喂，荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！这里这里，赶紧过来！
t_en: Hey, honorary member {NICKNAME}! Here, here, come here!

t_ch: 糟了，又是你…
t_en: Yikes, it's you again...

t_ch: 你那是什么态度啊？赶快打起精神来，这是命令！
t_en: What's that attitude? Sharpen up! And that is an order!

t_ch: 向你致敬，尤利安队长！
t_en: Hats off to you, Captain Julian!

t_ch: 啊？哦，那个…稍息！哎呀，你怎么突然表现得这么客气，我都不习惯啦！
t_en: Huh? Oh, okay... At ease! Er, why are you so polite all of a sudden, I'm not used to this!

t_ch: 你还没忘记「鼹鼠党振兴计划」吧？告诉你哦，根据我的线人报告，「地火」今天又要送很多份物资——这不正是鼹鼠党出动的好时机嘛！
t_en: You haven't forgotten The Moles Reinvigoration Plan, have you? Let me tell you, according to my informant's report, Wildfire needs to send lots of supplies today — isn't this a good time for The Moles to make a move?

t_ch: 你的「线人」是谁？
t_en: Who is your informant?

t_ch: 当然是阿…不对不对，我才不能告诉你！作为鼹鼠党的首席情报官，保护线人可是我的义务！
t_en: It's... Uh, no, I can't tell you! As the Chief Intelligence Officer of The Moles, it is my duty to protect the informant!

t_ch: 那个什么计划，我忘了…
t_en: What plan was that, I forgot...

t_ch: 可恶，大人的记性都这么差嘛？
t_en: Ugh, do all grown-ups have such bad memory?

t_ch: 那我就再给你解释一遍吧——「鼹鼠党振兴计划」就是要找到「地火」工作中的疏漏，然后靠我们敏捷的行动力把空缺补上！
t_en: Then I'll explain it to you again — The Moles Reinvigoration Plan is to find the work that Wildfire has neglected and take it up right away!

t_ch: 只要我们坚持这么做，鼹鼠党很快就能在下层区积攒名声，说不定有朝一日还能取代「地火」！嘿嘿……
t_en: If we keep doing this, The Moles will soon build a reputation in the Underworld, and maybe one day replace Wildfire! Hehe...

t_ch: 好了好了，别浪费时间了，我们快去「地火」的秘密集会所看看情况吧。
t_en: Alright, that's enough talk. Let's go to the secret gathering place of Wildfire and see what's going on.

t_ch: 唔…奥列格头儿之前说，今天的任务是给在矿区作业的矿队运送生活物资，但可没提过是要送两大箱东西啊……
t_en: Hmm... Boss Oleg said earlier that today's mission is to deliver daily necessities to the mining team, but he didn't mention it was two giant crates...

t_ch: 这么重，而且还是在不同的矿区…怎么办怎么办？斯薇塔，好好想想……
t_en: They are so heavy, and should be delivered to different mining areas... What should I do? Think, Swetta, think...

t_ch: …哎呀，不管了，还是用老办法吧！一二三四五，矿区打地鼠；地鼠跑得快，伤筋又动骨……
t_en: ...Ugh, whatever, I'll stick to my old trick! Eeny, meeny, miny, mole, catch a little mole by its toe. If he hollers, let him go, eeny, meeny, miny, mole...

t_ch: 好，就先送它了！哎，明天又要腰酸背疼了……
t_en: Well, I'll deliver this one first! Ugh, my back is gonna hurt tomorrow...

t_ch: 唔，原来大人也会用那么幼稚的方法做决定啊……
t_en: Hmm, so grown-ups would also make decisions this childishly...

t_ch: 快，{NICKNAME}！趁着斯薇塔姐姐刚走，我们赶紧过去看看剩下的那箱货物！
t_en: Quick, {NICKNAME}! Swetta just left, let's go check out the other box of goods!

t_ch: 这箱子上也写了配送地址，让我看看！
t_en: The shipping address is also written on the box. Let me take a look!

t_ch: 大矿区…伊蕾恩…哦，原来是伊蕾恩姐姐需要的物资！太好了，这下我就能拜托她帮忙宣传鼹鼠党的事迹啦。
t_en: The Great Mine... Elaine... Oh, these are the supplies Elaine needs! Great, now I can ask her to help spread word about The Moles' good deeds.

t_ch: 走吧，荣誉队员！我们去大矿区给伊蕾恩姐姐送物资！
t_en: Let's go, honorary member! Let's go to the Great Mine to deliver supplies to Elaine!

t_ch: 荣誉队员，看那边，伊蕾恩姐姐好像被一群坏家伙围住了！
t_en: Honorary member, look! Elaine seems to be surrounded by some bad people!

t_ch: 快点，{NICKNAME}！我们去教训那帮坏家伙一顿，把她给救出来！
t_en: Hurry, {NICKNAME}! Let's teach those bad guys a lesson and save her!

t_ch: 哼，那帮家伙，欺负女孩子的时候那么嚣张，其实根本不经打嘛。
t_en: Hmph, those shameless thugs only acted tough when they were bullying a girl.

t_ch: 伊蕾恩姐姐，你还好吗？
t_en: Elaine, are you okay?

t_ch: 我…我没事！谢谢你们，{NICKNAME}，还有尤利安……
t_en: I... I'm fine! Thank you, {NICKNAME}, and Julian...

t_ch: 你怎么总是被流浪者盯上…
t_en: Why are you always targeted by vagrants...

t_ch: 又被您救了一次，真是太不好意思了……
t_en: You saved me again. I can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 下次出来作业记得带个同伴。
t_en: Remember to bring a companion next time you go out for work.

t_ch: 我…我知道了，下次一定！又被您救了一次，实在是不好意思……
t_en: I... I will! You saved me again. I can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 那个…请问，您怎么会突然跑来大矿区，而且还是带着尤利安小朋友一起？
t_en: Em... if I may ask — why did you come to the Great Mine all of a sudden, with little Julian?

t_ch: 啊，差点把正事忘了！荣誉队员，赶紧把伊蕾恩姐姐的东西交给她吧。
t_en: Ah, I almost forgot! Honorary member, please give Elaine her stuff.

t_ch: 这是…我之前订的生活物资？
t_en: This is... the supplies I ordered earlier?

t_ch: 太、太感谢你们了！要是没有这箱东西，我今晚就只能穿着这身臭烘烘的工作服睡觉了。
t_en: Thank you so much! Without this box of supplies, I'd have to be sleeping in this smelly work suit tonight.

t_ch: 不过物资的派送不是一直由「地火」负责的吗？怎么今天轮到你们两个来了……
t_en: But isn't Wildfire usually responsible for deliveries? Why is it you two today?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，「地火」的家伙才不靠谱呢。告诉你吧伊蕾恩姐姐，以后这下层区就是鼹鼠党的天下啦——不管是打坏人还是送快递，都可以委托我们哦！
t_en: Hehe, the guys from Wildfire are not reliable. Let me tell you, Elaine — from now on, The Moles will take over the Underworld! You can entrust us with anything — beating up bad people or delivering packages, anything!

t_ch: 「鼹鼠党」？啊…我明白了！谢谢你，尤利安，鼹鼠党真是一群乐于助人的孩子。
t_en: The Moles? Ah... I see. Thank you, Julian, The Moles' members are all very good kids.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，小事小事！只要伊蕾恩姐姐帮我们把鼹鼠党的英勇事迹宣传出去就好啦。
t_en: Hehe, don't mention it! But you can spread the word about the heroic deeds of The Moles.

t_ch: 这次的行动也完美收官咯，{NICKNAME}！真不愧是虎克大人亲自任命的荣誉队员，不但跑得很快，战斗起来也很拉风嘛！
t_en: Good job, {NICKNAME}! You're really worth your salt as an honorary member appointed by Boss Hook — you act fast, and fight well!

t_ch: 多亏了咱们的努力，「鼹鼠党振兴计划」又向前迈出了一大步！喏，这些奖励你拿好了，以后还要继续帮尤利安打下手哦。
t_en: Thanks to our effort, The Moles Reinvigoration Plan has taken another big step forward! Here, take these rewards, and keep up the good work!

t_ch: 荣誉队员{NICKNAME}，这边，这边！
t_en: Honorary member {NICKNAME}, here!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，又被我撞到了！你每次出现的时机都刚刚好，简直太完美啦。
t_en: Hehe, I bumped into you again! You always show up at the right time. This is just perfect.

t_ch: 你是不是一直在跟踪我？
t_en: Have you been stalking me?

t_ch: 你、你在说啥呢？没有证据的话可不要瞎说哦……
t_en: Wh—What are you talking about? You can't just say things like that without any proof...

t_ch: 长官，有何吩咐？
t_en: Captain, what can I do for you?

t_ch: 稍息，荣誉队员！听我慢慢和你说……
t_en: At ease, honorary member! Let me tell you the details...

t_ch: 我从线人那里得到消息，「地火」今天又接到了一大批派送订单，他们的那点人手早就忙不过来啦。
t_en: I got news from my informant that Wildfire received a large number of delivery orders today, and has been overwhelmed by the workload.

t_ch: 根据我的英明判断，这可是「鼹鼠党」趁机介入的好机会，{NICKNAME}！要是我们能把他们来不及处理的订单都提前送到……
t_en: According to my wise judgment, this is a good opportunity for The Moles, {NICKNAME}! If we can deliver all the orders that they can't handle in time...

t_ch: …那「鼹鼠党振兴计划」的目标说不定就能提前达成咯，很棒吧？
t_en: ...Then the goal of The Moles Reinvigoration Plan might be achieved ahead of schedule, isn't it great?

t_ch: 我得了一种送快递上瘾的病…
t_en: I have an addiction to package delivery...

t_ch: 良性竞争是好事啊。
t_en: Healthy competition is always good.

t_ch: 唔，你好像很爱说些奇怪的话呢…你以为尤利安是小孩子就听不懂吗？
t_en: Hmm, you seem to like saying strange things... You think I won't understand just because I'm a kid?

t_ch: 好了好了，动嘴不如动腿，咱们快去「地火」的地盘上探探情报吧！
t_en: Alright alright, that's enough chat. Time to go to Wildfire's turf and gather some intel!

t_ch: 快看，荣誉队员！今天这里居然一个人都没有，我们运气真是太好了！
t_en: Look, honorary member! There is no one here today. We are in luck!

t_ch: 让我看看，这里有两箱物资，分别要送到<color=#dbc291ff>机械聚落</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>歌德大饭店</color>……
t_en: Let me see, there are two boxes of supplies to be delivered to <color=#dbc291ff>Robot Settlement</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>Goethe Grand Hotel</color> separately...

t_ch: 唔，之前「地火」的大人遇到这种情况，总是要纠结很久——也就是说，要是我们能把这两份物资都快速送达，那鼹鼠党就完成了「地火」做不到的事情…对吧？
t_en: Well, the grown-ups from Wildfire always waste a lot of time deciding which one to deliver first — so if we can make these two deliveries quickly, it would mean The Moles can achieve what Wildfire can't... right?

t_ch: 太好了，{NICKNAME}，咱们一定得把这件事办成！把两箱物资都拿上，先送哪份就由你来定吧！
t_en: Great, {NICKNAME}, we gotta get this done! Take both boxes of supplies, and you can decide which one to deliver first!

t_ch: 咦？怎么回事，你怎么一副很着急的样子？还有这位小朋友，为什么要跑来聚落……
t_en: Huh? What's wrong? Why do you look so anxious? And this kid, why do you come to the settlement...

t_ch: 我们是来送货的！{NICKNAME}，把菲多拉姐姐的物资拿给她吧。
t_en: We are here to deliver these goods! {NICKNAME}, give Fidora her supplies.

t_ch: 啊——这是我之前下单的机械零件！没想到这么快就送到了，「地火」的效率可真高啊。
t_en: Ah — these are the mechanical parts I ordered earlier! I didn't expect these to be delivered so quickly. Wildfire is really efficient.

t_ch: 不是「地火」，是鼹鼠党哦！
t_en: It's not Wildfire! It's The Moles!

t_ch: 姐姐你要记住哦，鼹鼠党才是下层区最厉害的组织！虎克老大会满足大家的所有要求！
t_en: Keep this in mind — The Moles is the best organization in the Underworld! Boss Hook will make sure all your needs are satisfied!

t_ch: 「鼹鼠党」…「虎克老大」？唔，这是什么过家家游戏吗？
t_en: The Moles... Boss Hook? Hmm, is this some kind of kid's game?

t_ch: 你就顺着这小鬼的话说吧…
t_en: Just play along...

t_ch: 不许你这么评价鼹鼠党！
t_en: You can't badmouth The Moles like that!

t_ch: …咳咳，我知道了，我收回刚才的话。
t_en: ...Ahem, I see, I take back what I just said.

t_ch: 辛苦你们啦！以后我要是遇上什么麻烦，肯定会记得找「鼹鼠党」帮忙的。
t_en: Thank you so much! If I encounter any problems in the future, I will definitely remember to ask The Moles for help.

t_ch: 歌蒂，你的快递。
t_en: Gertie, your package.

t_ch: 哎呀，这不是我之前包给熔锤镇老铁匠的那批餐具吗？本来约定的交货时间是下个星期，没想到这么快就送到了…谢谢你们专门为此跑一趟。
t_en: Oh, it's the tableware order I placed! I thought it wouldn't be delivered until next week... Thank you for making a special trip for this.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不用谢，歌蒂阿姨！记住，以后再有需要帮忙的地方，可以随时召唤鼹鼠党哦！
t_en: Hehe, you are welcome, Ms. Gertie! Remember, you can come to The Moles for help any time!

t_ch: 「鼹鼠党」…？啊，我明白了，是奥列格之前提到过的……
t_en: The Moles...? Ah, I see, Oleg once told me about this...

t_ch: …奥列格说什么了？
t_en: What did Oleg say?

t_ch: 啊，没什么，别在意！既然餐具这么早就到了，饭店的环境翻新日程也该提前了，哈哈。
t_en: Oh, nothing! Since the tableware arrived so early, the renovation schedule of the hotel should also be put ahead, haha.

t_ch: 谢谢你们，{NICKNAME}还有尤利安。有关「鼹鼠党」的事迹，我肯定会多跟客人们提及的。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME} and Julian. I will definitely tell the guests about the good deeds of The Moles.

t_ch: 真的吗？那就全靠你了，歌蒂阿姨！
t_en: Really? Thank you, Ms. Gertie!

t_ch: 呼…今天的任务也顺利完成啦！有歌蒂阿姨帮忙，用不了几天，鼹鼠党就能取代「地火」，成为下层区最受欢迎的组织了吧？
t_en: Phew... Today's mission accomplished! With Ms. Gertie's help, The Moles will be able to replace Wildfire soon and become the most popular organization in the Underworld, right?

t_ch: 唔…这么一想，我们的人手会不会不够呢？得想个办法，把整个磐岩镇——不对，是要把整个下层的小孩子全都纳入鼹鼠党的旗下，嘿嘿！
t_en: Hmm... in that case, we probably need more people. We gotta figure out a way to recruit all the kids in Boulder Town — no, the whole of Underworld, hehe!

t_ch: 就这么决定了，下一步的行动计划，就起名叫「鼹鼠党极速扩张作战」吧！
t_en: So that's it, the next action plan — The Moles Rapid Expansion Operation!

t_ch: 干得不错，荣誉队员{NICKNAME}——以后你也要多多支持本情报官的工作哦！今天就到这里吧，鼹鼠党，解散！
t_en: Well done, honorary member {NICKNAME} — please keep supporting my job as an intelligence officer in the future! Let's call it a day. The Moles, dismiss!

t_ch: 刚才那个小鬼…是叫尤利安对吧？这几天来真是有劳你们俩了，哈哈哈。
t_en: That kid just now... is called Julian, right? The two of you have been really helpful these past few days, hahaha.

t_ch: 这是怎么回事？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: 我就知道事情没这么简单…
t_en: I knew this whole thing wasn't how it looked...

t_ch: 你也知道，下层区没有铁卫管束，很多民间事务只能靠「地火」出面解决，其中就包括生活必要物资的分装、派送。
t_en: You know, there are no Silvermane Guards in the Underworld, and many civil affairs can only be handled by Wildfire, including the distribution and delivery of basic necessities.

t_ch: 你应该懂吧？派送这活需要消耗大量的人力，光凭我手下的那些人，就算日夜连轴转也没法保证整个下层区的物资供应。
t_en: You get it, right? Delivery requires a lot of hands. Even if my people work <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7, they couldn't guarantee the supply of the whole of Underworld.

t_ch: 正巧，「鼹鼠党」的那群孩子都是个顶个的热心。有他们掺和这么一下子，我们本来紧张的运力反而能缓解不少了。
t_en: Luckily, the kids of The Moles are very caring and helpful. Their involvement actually helps a lot.

t_ch: 真是老谋深算啊…
t_en: You're so crafty...

t_ch: 被算计了啊，尤利安！
t_en: So Julian was just being used!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，先把话说明白咯，这可不是我出的主意啊。要我猜的话…指定是娜塔莎跟虎克说了什么悄悄话吧？
t_en: Hahaha, let's make it clear first, this is not my idea. If I had to guess... it must be Natasha who told Hook some "secrets."

t_ch: 总而言之，看在我的面子上，这事你可要跟「鼹鼠党」的孩子们保密啊——就让他们继续保持这股子乐于助人的热情吧。
t_en: Anyway, for my sake, please keep this a secret from The Moles kids — just let them keep up their enthusiasm for helping others.

t_ch: 荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！快过来快过来，又有新任务啦！
t_en: Hey, honorary member {NICKNAME}! Quick, come here! We have a new mission!

t_ch: 「振兴计划」已经进行到最关键的攻坚阶段了！我们必须发扬虎克老大提倡的鼹鼠党精神，一鼓作气，埋头苦干，坚定不移！
t_en: The Moles' Reinvigoration Plan has reached a critical stage! We must carry forward the spirit of the Moles advocated by Boss Hook! Now's no time to waver! March onward!

t_ch: …你都哪儿学的成语？
t_en: ...Seems you learned a lot of new mottos lately.

t_ch: 只是复述虎克老大的原话而已——这才不是重点！
t_en: That's beside the point! The point is to recite the original words spoken by Boss Hook!

t_ch: …作气！…苦干！…不移！
t_en: No time... Hard work... Onward!

t_ch: 不愧是荣誉队员，要的就是你这股劲！
t_en: Not bad for an honorary member! That's the spirit!

t_ch: 我的小弟之前得到消息，「地火」又积攒了一批需要递送的货物，但因为人手紧张还没能发货。
t_en: My source told me earlier that Wildfire just accumulated another batch of goods that needs to be delivered, but they haven't been able to ship them out because of staffing constraints.

t_ch: 这不正是我们的机会吗？快，跟我去「地火」的集会所看看情况！
t_en: Isn't this our chance? Come on, let's go to Wildfire's Gathering Place and see what's going on!

t_ch: 这都是「地火」的阴谋…
t_en: This is all a scheme by Wildfire...

t_ch: 啊？什么阴谋，你别在那胡思乱想啦！
t_en: What? Scheming? That's quite the imagination you've got there.

t_ch: 走了走了，得趁着「地火」反应过来之前赶紧行动！
t_en: Let's go! We have to act quickly before Wildfire has a chance to react!

t_ch: 带路吧。
t_en: Lead the way then.

t_ch: 走吧，得赶在「地火」反应过来之前行动！
t_en: Let's get on with it. We have to act quickly before Wildfire has a chance to react!

t_ch: 快看，荣誉队员！「地火」要送的快递果然都放在这里。
t_en: Honorary member, look! It's the supplies Wildfire is planning to ship out.

t_ch: 唔，居然堆积了这么多…这帮大人到底行不行啊！
t_en: Oh, there's so much stuff piled up here... Can we really rely on these people?

t_ch: 让我看看——有要送到娜塔莎姐姐的诊所，有送到大矿区的，还有要送到机械聚落的……
t_en: Let's see — some are headed to Natasha's clinic, some to the Great Mine, and some are going to the Robot Settlement...

t_ch: 哇…待派送的快递实在太多了！只靠咱们两个恐怕一整天都送不完……
t_en: Wow... there are so many deliveries to be made! I'm afraid that just the two of us won't be able to deliver them all even if we work for the whole day.

t_ch: 唔，我还答应了阿丽娜，今天晚上要带她去吃火激凌…荣誉队员{NICKNAME}，我们今天就选一份快递派送吧！要送哪份就交给你决定！
t_en: Well, I promised Alina that I'd take her out for Fire Chillin' Ice Cream tonight... So, honorary member {NICKNAME}, let's pick one package to deliver today! I'll leave it up to you to decide which one!

t_ch: 派送匹克的快递。
t_en: Deliver Peak's package.

t_ch: 收件人是匹克…哦，就是那个一直睡懒觉的矿工叔叔吧？
t_en: The recipient is Peak... Oh, I guess is the miner who is always sleeping in.

t_ch: 收件地址是大矿区…哇，他今天居然去干活了？真是难得一见。
t_en: The delivery address is the Great Mine... So he went to work today for real? How peculiar.

t_ch: 走吧，我们去大矿区找他！
t_en: Let's go find him in the Great Mine.

t_ch: 派送伊妮丝的快递。
t_en: Deliver Eunice's package.

t_ch: 收件人是…伊妮丝？
t_en: The recipient is... Eunice?

t_ch: 唔…我好像听虎克老大说过这个名字，好像是个住在机械聚落的怪姐姐……
t_en: Umm... I think I've heard Boss Hook mentioning this name before. She's a quirky girl living in the Robot Settlement if I remember correctly...

t_ch: 听说她得了一种「凡是说话就必须押韵」的怪病…正好！趁着这次机会，我也要去见识一下！
t_en: I heard that she developed a strange condition where she constantly speaks in rhyme. I'd say this is a perfect chance for us to check it out.

t_ch: 派送某个病人的快递。
t_en: Deliver the patient's package.

t_ch: 收件人是…哇，字迹歪歪扭扭的，完全看不清楚！
t_en: The recipient is... ugh, the handwriting is terrible. I can't figure it out!

t_ch: 地址倒是能勉强看清…是娜塔莎姐姐的诊所！让我猜猜，这快递应该是要送给某个病人的吧？
t_en: The delivery address is still readable... Natasha's clinic! Let me guess. Could it be sent to a certain patient?

t_ch: 走吧，我们去诊所问问看——嘿嘿，这下说不定还能得到娜塔莎姐姐的表扬……
t_en: Let's go ask around in the clinic. Hehe, a helpful kid like me might even earn Natasha's praise...

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 喂，醒醒！匹克叔叔——醒醒！
t_en: Hey, Wake up! Peak, wake up!

t_ch: 呼…呼噜…呼噜……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 哇，睡得也太沉了吧！换作是我，肯定没法在这么硬的地上睡着……
t_en: Oh, he's sound asleep! If I were him, I definitely can't sleep on such a hard floor...

t_ch: 需要我把他揍醒吗？
t_en: You want me to kick him up?

t_ch: …不必不必！看他睡得这么熟，肯定是采矿的工作太累了吧？
t_en: ...No, no! He must have been tired from mining and needs a good rest.

t_ch: 你的嗓门还不够大。
t_en: You should raise your voice.

t_ch: …算了算了！看他睡得这么熟，肯定是采矿的工作太累了吧？
t_en: ...Never mind! He must have been tired from mining and needs a good rest.

t_ch: 就把快递包裹放他旁边吧——哦对，记得在上面写上鼹鼠党的名字！
t_en: Let's leave his package next to him. Oh right, don't forget to put The Moles' name on it!

t_ch: …好啦，这样就行了。希望他醒来以后能记得把包裹带上……
t_en: ...Okay, this should do it. Hope he still remembers he has a package when he wakes up.

t_ch: 多谢你的帮助，荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！「鼹鼠党振兴计划」今天又向前迈出了一大步，这都多亏了你的努力…还有我的周密计划！
t_en: Thanks for your help, honorary member {NICKNAME}. The Moles' Reinvigoration Plan has taken another big step forward. This is the fruit of your contribution... as well as my careful planning!

t_ch: 用不了多久，有关咱们的英雄事迹就会传遍下层区了…怎么样，是不是很期待？
t_en: Before long, our legendary story will be told throughout the Underworld. Don't you feel excited!?

t_ch: 下次任务再见啦，荣誉队员——鼹鼠党，解散！
t_en: I'll see you at our next mission, honorary member. The Moles, dismiss!

t_ch: …唔，{NICKNAME}，这个姐姐为什么不说话？
t_en: ...Umm, {NICKNAME}, why isn't she saying anything?

t_ch: 说唱歌手是这样的。
t_en: That's how a rapper is.

t_ch: 说唱歌手…哇，好酷！尤利安也想成为酷酷的说唱歌手！
t_en: A rapper... That sounds so cool! I want to become a cool rapper, too!

t_ch: …喂，你怎么也不说话了！
t_en: ...Hey, why aren't you saying anything either?

t_ch: 咳，算啦！不想说话也没什么。{NICKNAME}，快把伊妮丝姐姐的快递交给她吧。
t_en: Huh... Never mind! I won't blame you for keeping quiet, {NICKNAME}. Just hand over Eunice's package to her.

t_ch: 姐姐拿好，这是你的包裹！切记，是鼹鼠党把它交到你手上的哦——下层区最酷的鼹鼠党！
t_en: Your package, sis, there you go! Remember! The coolest The Moles delivered it over to you.
...Huh?

t_ch: 哟哟——耶，站稳，听好！这里是八十八度矿底的伊妮丝——今天要把发自内心的赞美，献给这个乐于助人的小鬼！
t_en: Yo, yo, listen up! Eunice from <unbreak>88</unbreak> Degrees Bedrock. Here's something for this kind and warm little kid. Respect from the deep of my heart!

t_ch: 喔、喔哦，要开始了吗？！我、我还没准备好呢——
t_en: Wow, yes, the rap is gonna start!? I—I'm not ready yet!

t_ch: 下层的野兽用激情点燃午夜，像骄傲的鼹鼠用辛勤对抗毁灭，
t_en: Light up the night with the fervor of the Undergrounds. Face up the destruction with toil like the pride moles.

t_ch: 不惧前路，不耍态度，只为每个孤单无助的灵魂都能被在乎，
t_en: Show no fear. Bring no veneer. Take care of the lost souls that are here.

t_ch: 无所谓，不去理会，信念的破碎又或是懒惰的罪，
t_en: Whatever the pain, however they complain. With shattered faith led astray from the lane, sinful indolence is the ultimate drain.

t_ch: 哪怕壮烈的「地火」不再如太阳般常燃——
t_en: Even if the valiant Wildfire would rise no more with the morning sun...

t_ch: 生而好强的鼹鼠也绝不妥协于黑暗！耶~
t_en: No born brave moles would ever yield to the darkness none!

t_ch: …喔…喔哦！太酷了，说唱实在是太酷了！
t_en: Wow... oh! That's amazing! The rap is so cool!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，还好你选了这份快递！我以后也要多跟伊妮丝姐姐学习，然后当上鼹鼠党最酷的说唱歌手！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm so glad that you picked this package to deliver! I'll learn from sister Eunice and become the best rapper of The Moles!
...Yeah.

t_ch: 多谢你的帮助，荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！「鼹鼠党振兴计划」今天又向前迈出了一大步，这都多亏了你的努力…还有我的周密计划！
t_en: Thanks for your help, honorary member {NICKNAME}. The Moles' Reinvigoration Plan has taken another big step forward. This is the fruit of your contribution... as well as my careful planning!

t_ch: 用不了多久，有关咱们的英雄事迹就会传遍下层区了…怎么样，是不是很期待？
t_en: Before long, our legendary story will be told throughout the Underworld. Don't you feel excited!?

t_ch: 下次任务再见啦，荣誉队员——鼹鼠党，解散！
t_en: I'll see you at our next mission, honorary member. The Moles, dismiss!

t_ch: 晕…好晕……
t_en: Dizzy... I feel so dizzy...

t_ch: 哎哟喂…我的头好晕……
t_en: Oh my, my head is so dizzy...

t_ch: …奶奶，我们这儿有你的快递！
t_en: ...Grandma, here's a package for you!

t_ch: 晕…有人在跟我说话吗？哎哟…我的药怎么还不来……
t_en: Oh... Is someone talking to me? Ah my medicine... when can it arrive...

t_ch: 药…啊，难道老奶奶的包裹里放的是治头晕的药吗？
t_en: Medicine... Oh, maybe her medicine is in this package?

t_ch: 荣誉队员，快把快递交给老奶奶吧！
t_en: Honorary member, hand over the package to grandma now!

t_ch: 老奶奶，拿好！快把药先吃了吧！
t_en: Grandma, take it! Have your medicine!

t_ch: 咕嘟…唔…好多了，这下好多了……
t_en: *glug* Umm... Good, I'm feeling better now...

t_ch: 谢谢你，孩子…还有你，年轻人。要是没有你们，这头晕的毛病怕不是要把我折磨个半死…唉……
t_en: Thank you, kid... And you, young one. My head would be torn apart by the dizziness without your help... *sigh*

t_ch: 这毛病跟了我好多年了，吃多少药都根治不了。娜塔莎医生这儿的特效药都要被我吃光了，她只能通过特殊渠道帮我找药……
t_en: This ailment has been with me for many years, and no matter how many medications I take, it never fully goes away. I've almost used up all of Doctor Natasha's stock to the point where she has to source more medicines for me through special channels.

t_ch: 老奶奶…你就好好在这儿休息吧！鼹鼠党记住你的难处了——以后我们也会经常给你送药的。
t_en: Grandma... have a good rest here! The Moles have heard about your problem, and we will continue delivering medicines to you from now on.

t_ch: 有娜塔莎姐姐在，你的头晕病一定能被治好的！一定要相信娜塔莎姐姐……
t_en: Natasha will definitely heal your dizziness! Have faith in her...

t_ch: 谢谢你，小朋友。啊，鼹鼠党可真是一群好孩子啊……
t_en: Thank you, kid. Oh, The Moles are a group of lovely children...

t_ch: 多谢你的帮助，荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！「鼹鼠党振兴计划」今天又向前迈出了一大步，这都多亏了你的努力…还有我的周密计划！
t_en: Thanks for your help, honorary member {NICKNAME}. The Moles' Reinvigoration Plan has taken another big step forward. This is the fruit of your contribution... as well as my careful planning!

t_ch: 用不了多久，有关咱们的英雄事迹就会传遍下层区了…怎么样，是不是很期待？
t_en: Before long, our legendary story will be told throughout the Underworld. Don't you feel excited!?

t_ch: 下次任务再见啦，荣誉队员——鼹鼠党，解散！
t_en: I'll see you at our next mission, honorary member. The Moles, dismiss!

t_ch: 老奶奶吃下了快递包裹里的头晕药……
t_en: Grandma had the medicines contained in the package...

t_ch: 无人派送的快递
t_en: Undelivered packages

t_ch: 与神情焦虑的老铁卫搭话…
t_en: Start a conversation with the anxious old guard...

t_ch: 矿石封装检查，完成…每周军备例行维护和清洁，完成……
t_en: Ore pack inspection, checked...Weekly armament routine maintenance and cleaning, checked...

t_ch: 又一天过去了，唉……
t_en: Another day... *sigh*

t_ch: 你为什么叹气？
t_en: What's with the sighing?

t_ch: 唉，又是平平淡淡的一天……
t_en: Alas, it's just another day...

t_ch: 能帮我擦擦鞋子吗？
t_en: Can you clean my shoes?

t_ch: …怎么说我也是铁卫的后勤官，居然要被你这种小年轻嘲笑，唉……
t_en: ... As the logistics officer of the Guard, I can't believe I am actually laughed at by a young {F#lady}{M#man} like you, alas...

t_ch: 等会儿，站住——你是谁？难道是非法闯入者？
t_en: Wait a minute, stop — who are you? Are you an intruder?

t_ch: 向吉尔伯特展示禁区通行证。
t_en: Show Gilbert the restricted zone pass.

t_ch: 哦，原来你有凭证啊…可惜，还以为我终于能在信里写些特别的事了……
t_en: Oh, so you have a pass... What a pity, I thought I could finally write something special in the letter...

t_ch: 信？
t_en: Letter?

t_ch: 没、没什么，你就当没听见吧……
t_en: No, nothing, just pretend you didn't hear it...

t_ch: 如果没有凭证，你打算怎么办？
t_en: What were you going to do if I didn't have a pass?

t_ch: 没、没打算干什么，不骗你，真的。
t_en: No, I... I didn't intend on doing anything... and I'm not lying to you now.

t_ch: 等、等等！那个…我看你虽然年纪不大，倒是摆出了一副饱经世事的神态嘛？能不能帮我这老兵一个忙？
t_en: W—Wait! Hmm... You look young, but your face seems to suggest you are quite experienced. Can you do this old soldier a favor?

t_ch: 实话和你说吧，我的役期还有几个月到头，很快我就能离开这片禁区了。我想写信把这好消息告诉老婆和女儿，但实在下不去笔……
t_en: To tell you the truth, I am only a few months from the end of my service, then I'll be leaving this restricted zone soon. I want to write a letter to tell my wife and daughter the good news, but I really don't know what to write about...

t_ch: 我在部队里服役二十来年，期间一直都在后勤工作，一次前线都没上过。开头的几年我还觉得这工作安逸舒坦，但越往后越觉得自己真是窝囊透顶……
t_en: I have served in the army for over <unbreak>20</unbreak> years, and I have been working in logistics this whole time. I have never been on the front line once. In the first few years, I still thought this job was easy and comfortable, but the longer I stay, the more useless I feel...

t_ch: 那个，你要是不介意的话，能不能和我分享些刺激的冒险经历？我不想让女儿觉得她老爹是个没本事的老家伙，但自己的想象力也实在有限……
t_en: Well, if you don't mind, can you share some exciting adventures with me? I don't want my daughter to think that her dad is a useless old bum, but my imagination is really limited...

t_ch: 好吧，让我想想……
t_en: Alright, let me think about it...

t_ch: 真的可以吗？谢、谢谢你，年轻人！
t_en: Really? Thank, thank you, {F#young lady}{M#young man}!

t_ch: 我的纸和笔呢…啊，这儿呢，找到了——行了，等你准备好了我们就开始……
t_en: Where's my paper and pen... ah, here, found it — okay, we can start when you're ready...

t_ch: 你整理好思路了？开始吧，你来说，我来记……
t_en: Have you made up your mind? Let's get started, you speak, I will write it down...

t_ch: 我想想…就从旅行的经历开始吧？你到过哪些特别的地方，和我说说吧。
t_en: Let me think... let's start with a traveling experience? Tell me about any special places you've been to.

t_ch: 城郊雪原…
t_en: Outlying Snow Plains...

t_ch: …雪原？那可是禁地啊，普通人擅自出城可是重罪一桩……
t_en: ... Snow Plains? That place is forbidden, and it's a serious crime for ordinary people to leave the city without authorization...

t_ch: 下层区的铆钉镇…
t_en: Rivet Town in the Underworld...

t_ch: …下层区？不可能不可能，上下层区都封锁十来年了，这么说没人会信的…
t_en: ... The Underworld? No way, no way, the passage between the surface and the underground has been blocked for more than ten years. No one will believe it if I say so...

t_ch: 湛蓝星…
t_en: The Blue...

t_ch: …什么玩意？完全没听过的地名啊，你不会是在胡编吧……
t_en: ...Where's that? I have never heard of that place. Are you just making it up?

t_ch: 哎，要是照你说的写，就算是一家人恐怕也很难置信吧？我还是改得稍微务实一点儿吧。
t_en: *sigh* If I write it as you said, it would be hard even for my family to believe me, right? I'd rather be a little more convincing.

t_ch: 那个…接下来聊聊别的吧，你懂作战吗？我虽然服了二十多年役，但是一次前线都没上过，武器也用的不怎么顺手。
t_en: Then... Let's talk about something else, do you know how to fight? Although I have served for more than <unbreak>20</unbreak> years, I have never been on the front line once, and I am not very good at using weapons.

t_ch: 你要是有过什么难忘的作战经历，就给我讲讲吧……
t_en: If you have any unforgettable combat experiences, tell me about them...

t_ch: 我和杰帕德切磋过两次…
t_en: I've fought against Gepard twice...

t_ch: 杰、杰帕德？！就是那个年纪轻轻就当上戍卫官的……
t_en: Ge—Gepard!? The young Captain...?

t_ch: 这…这写出来谁信啊？算了，我还是写点稍微靠谱的东西吧。
t_en: This... Who would believe this? Forget it, I'll just write something a little more realistic.

t_ch: 我和叫史瓦罗的机器人对战过…
t_en: I fought against a robot named Svarog...

t_ch: 机器人？唔…这好像也没什么特别的啊，我也经常要负责修理禁区里的故障机器人……
t_en: Robot? Hmm... it doesn't sound special, I'm often in charge of repairing malfunctioning robots in the Restricted Zone...

t_ch: 哎，我还是稍微添油加醋一下吧。
t_en: *sigh* I will just embellish the story a bit.

t_ch: 我和布洛妮娅打了个平手…
t_en: Bronya and I fought to a draw...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅？！是、是我想到的那个布洛妮娅吗？
t_en: Bronya? ! Is it the Bronya I am thinking of?

t_ch: 这…这写出来谁信啊？算了，我还是写点稍微靠谱的东西吧。
t_en: This... Who would believe this? Forget it, I'll just write something a little more realistic.

t_ch: 我算缓过劲来了——你是不是一直在逗我玩呢？你觉得这很好笑吗？
t_en: I get it now — have you been playing with me? Do you think this is funny?

t_ch: 哎，现在的年轻人，对老一辈真是一点都不尊重…不过话说回来，要是没有你提供的思路，我这封信怕是等到明年也下不去笔。
t_en: *sigh* Young people nowadays really have no respect for the elders at all... But then again, without the ideas you mentioned, I'm afraid I won't be able to write this letter until next year.

t_ch: …这些你拿着吧，就当是耽误时间的赔礼了。
t_en: ...Anyway, thanks for stopping by. Here, for your trouble.

t_ch: 莉拉…你可千万不要看不起老爹啊，哎。
t_en: Lila... Please don't be ashamed of Daddy! *sigh*

t_ch: …我当然明白你的想法，吉尔伯特。但是这战功勋章可不是我想发给谁就发给谁的啊。
t_en: ...I certainly get your point, Gilbert. But this medal of merit is not something I can give to anyone I want.

t_ch: 你在部队里待了多久了，二十年？三十年？这点道理难道还不明白吗……
t_en: How long have you been in the army, twenty years? Thirty years? Don't you understand this...

t_ch: 邓恩啊，你还是个新兵蛋子的时候我就特别照顾你，怎么当上队长以后就把架子端起来了呢？
t_en: Dunn, I took special care of you when you were a new recruit. Now that you've become a captain, how come you are putting on airs with me?

t_ch: 这么个小忙你都不肯帮我，是想看我被老婆和女儿嘲笑的样子吗……
t_en: Can't you do me this tiny little favor? Do you want to see me getting laughed at by my wife and daughter...?

t_ch: 我都跟你说了好多遍了，这是原则问题……
t_en: I've told you many times. It's a matter of principle...

t_ch: …啊，正好，{NICKNAME}来了！这件事你来评评理吧。
t_en: ...Ah, right on time, here comes {NICKNAME}! What do you make of his request?

t_ch: 其实我不想多管闲事…
t_en: Actually, I don't want to meddle...

t_ch: …你别装了，我早就知道你是个乐于助人的家伙。
t_en: ...Come on now, you never shy away from giving people a little help.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 你认识吉尔伯特吧？对，就是这位，禁区资历最老的后勤官。
t_en: Do you know Gilbert? Yes, him, the oldest logistics officer in the restricted zone.

t_ch: 他的役期马上就要到了，要我说，只要安安稳稳度过这几个月，等着回城里去和妻女团圆就行了……
t_en: He's about to retire. The way I see it, he should spend the last few months doing what he's been doing and return to the city to reunite with his wife and daughter when he finally retires...

t_ch: 可他偏要在这时候跟我提无理的要求！这老头想借一枚战功勋章，就为了能在家里人面前涨涨面子。你说说，这合理吗？
t_en: But he wants to ask an unreasonable favor from me at a time like this! The geezer wants to borrow a medal of merit, just to look good in front of his family. You tell me, is that reasonable?

t_ch: 勋章不能外借吗？
t_en: Can't you just lend him the medal?

t_ch: 当然不能！你把银鬃铁卫的规章当成什么了？
t_en: Of course not! What do you think the rules of the Silvermane Guards are for?

t_ch: 这么做确实太儿戏了。
t_en: True... It doesn't sound very appropriate.

t_ch: 你看吧，老头！连{NICKNAME}也这么说。
t_en: See, old man! Even {NICKNAME} said so.

t_ch: 再说了，你不是有服役满二十周年的铁卫老兵勋章吗？这荣誉的分量难道还不够你去炫耀的吗……
t_en: Besides, don't you have the Veteran Guard Medal from your twentieth anniversary? Isn't that enough honor for you to show off...

t_ch: 年轻人，你怎么不懂呢…这种只需要熬资历就能得到的东西才算不上什么荣誉，只会更被人看不起……
t_en: Youngster, why don't you understand... You can get one of these just by staying long enough. It is not considered an honor. And it won't bring me any respect...

t_ch: 哎，跟你真是说不通……
t_en: *sigh* You just don't get it, do you?

t_ch: 我能帮上什么忙吗？
t_en: Can I help with anything?

t_ch: 对啊，让我想想……
t_en: Yeah, let me think about it...

t_ch: 怎么没人给我发勋章呢？
t_en: Why is no one giving me any medal?

t_ch: …你不记得把我打晕的那回事了？你的这种情况最多算是戴罪立功吧……
t_en: ...Remember that time you knocked me out? Consider yourself lucky that you're not sent to court-martial yet, let alone getting a medal...

t_ch: 等等，你这倒是提醒了我……
t_en: Wait, you reminded me...

t_ch: 有了，我想到一个好办法！
t_en: Yes, I have a good idea!

t_ch: 听说最近又有一头「灰熊」自动机兵失控了，现在还在禁区里游荡破坏。现在人手短缺，我的部下只能暂时控制住它周边的区域，不敢妄自行动。
t_en: I heard that another automaton "Grizzly" has lost control recently, and is still roaming and wreaking havoc in the restricted zone. Now that there is a shortage of personnel, my subordinates can only maintain control in the surrounding area temporarily and they dare not act rashly.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你能带着老头儿去解决掉这个麻烦吗？只要干成了这件事，战功我可以记在吉尔伯特的名下。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, can you take the old man to deal with this problem? When this is done, I can give the credit to Gilbert.

t_ch: 还、还有这么凑巧的好事？年、年轻人，走，我们快去看看情况吧！
t_en: Wh—what a great opportunity! {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, let's go and take a look!

t_ch: 呜…呜哇！原来是这么巨大的机器人吗？
t_en: Wh—Whoa! That's one big robot!

t_ch: 你难道没见过这种型号吗…
t_en: You've never seen a model like this before...?

t_ch: 呃…整天待在后勤区域里，这些先进的机兵武器本来就没什么机会接触到啊。
t_en: Uh... I spend all of my time on the logistics side of things. I don't really get any chances to see an advanced mechanical war machine like this.

t_ch: 对我来说就是小菜一碟啦。
t_en: It's nothing I can't handle.

t_ch: 你、你这么有信心？
t_en: Y—You're that confident?

t_ch: 那个…要跟它来硬的吗？我……
t_en: So... You're going to fight that thing? I...

t_ch: 咳…我准备好了！你们先上吧，我、我会尽全力协助你们的！
t_en: Ahem... I'm ready! You guys go first. I—I'll support you from behind!

t_ch: 哈！胜、胜利了！这大家伙，看着吓人，实际上外强中干嘛！
t_en: Haha! V—Victory! This fella might look scary, but he's actually not so tough!

t_ch: 你其实什么也没干吧…
t_en: You didn't do anything...

t_ch: 怎么能这么说？作、作战基础一零一，场外支援同样十分重要……
t_en: How could you say that? I—It's Combat Basics <unbreak>101</unbreak>! Support off the battlefield is of equal importance...

t_ch: 打得漂亮，吉尔伯特！
t_en: Well done, Gilbert!

t_ch: 你是在挖苦我吗？我告诉你，作、作战基础一零一，场外支援也是非常重要的……
t_en: What's with the sarcasm? Listen here, i—it's Combat Basics <unbreak>101</unbreak>! Support off the battlefield is of equal importance...

t_ch: 快，我们快点回去找邓恩吧，别让他等不耐烦了。
t_en: Come on, let's hurry back and talk to Dunn. We shouldn't keep him waiting.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，吉尔伯特，你们回来了！我已经收到铁卫的汇报了，任务圆满完成。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Gilbert, you're back! I received a report from the Guards that your mission was a success.

t_ch: 那…我的战功勋章是不是……
t_en: Then... that means my medal of service...

t_ch: 当然了，老头儿——我已经帮你把申报手续走完了，最快下周就能收到消息。
t_en: Of course, old man — I've already finished the paperwork for your medal's application. We should hear back from them next week or so.

t_ch: 真、真的吗？太好了……
t_en: R—Really? That's great...

t_ch: 我就知道，以前没白关照你这个新兵蛋子！那、那我就回后勤单元等你的好消息了。
t_en: I knew I didn't waste my time taking care of the new generation of soldiers! W—Well then, I'll return to the logistics unit and wait for the good news from you.

t_ch: …也谢谢你，年轻人。要是没有你，我恐怕就要沦为笑柄了……
t_en: ...And thank you as well, youngster. If not for you, I would've been doomed to be a laughingstock...

t_ch: 哎，那个老头儿……
t_en: *sigh* That old man...

t_ch: 他是在跟自己过不去…
t_en: He's too hard on himself...

t_ch: 你和我想的一样。这么多年没见过面的家人，哪儿会在意什么战功勋章呢？
t_en: That's what I was thinking. After so many years of being apart, his family would not be concerned with these military accolades.

t_ch: 他的家人有那么势利眼吗？
t_en: Is his family that judgmental?

t_ch: 我没见过他的妻子和孩子，不过…我觉得她们不会在意什么战功勋章。
t_en: I haven't met his wife or child, but... I think they won't care about whether or not he has a medal.

t_ch: 不过这泡沫也轮不到我们这些外人来戳破，对吧？毕竟那是他的家事。
t_en: But it's not our place to burst his bubble, is it? This is between him and his family.

t_ch: 答应我一件事，{NICKNAME}——如果吉尔伯特以后还来找你帮忙的话，尽量答应他的要求，行吗？那老头是个好人，我不想看到他在这最后几个月里受委屈。
t_en: Promise me something, {NICKNAME} — if Gilbert comes to you with another request in the future, do your best to help, okay? He's a good man. I don't want to see him suffer during his last few months of service.

t_ch: 哦，对了，这些你拿去吧。我知道打倒故障机器人都是你的功劳，总不能让你白跑一趟，是吧？
t_en: Oh right, take these. I know you did all the work in taking down that malfunctioning robot. I can't let you leave empty-handed, now can I?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，年轻人！终于等到你了，我还怕你以后都不会来禁区了……
t_en: {NICKNAME}! I've been waiting for you, youngster. I was afraid that you might never come to the restricted zone...

t_ch: 又见面了，老头子。
t_en: Nice seeing you again, old man.

t_ch: 我还有别的事…
t_en: I have other things to do...

t_ch: 一分钟，我就耽误你一分钟！当我求你了…
t_en: One minute, just one minute of your time! I'm begging you...

t_ch: 那个，你还记得我想寄给老婆女儿的信吗？我昨天总算把它给写完了。
t_en: Uh, do you remember that letter I wanted to send to my wife and daughter? I finished writing it yesterday.

t_ch: 你要是不介意的话，能不能替我把信送到女儿手里？禁区的寄送手续太繁杂了，要通过好几层审核…怕是没等到信寄出去，我这役期就已经满了。
t_en: If you don't mind, could you help take it to my daughter for me? It's an arduous process mailing things from inside the restricted zone. You need one approval after another... The letter would probably not even be sent before my service was up.

t_ch: 拜托了，年轻人！我认识的人里，能自由出入这禁区的就你这一个。要是连你都不肯帮忙，那我就真要绝望了！
t_en: Please, youngster! You're the only person I know who is able to freely move in and out of the restricted zone. If you don't help, then there is no hope for me to get this letter delivered!

t_ch: 我女儿名叫莉拉，她和她老妈就住在行政区。这事就有劳你了，年轻人……
t_en: My daughter's name is Lila. She and her mother live in the Administrative District. Thanks for doing this, youngster...

t_ch: 你是吉尔伯特的女儿吗？
t_en: Are you Gilbert's daughter?

t_ch: 欸？你们认识我老爸？
t_en: Eh? You guys know my father?

t_ch: 这有一封你父亲给你的信。
t_en: This is a letter from your father.

t_ch: 这不是什么新的诈骗技巧吧？他工作的地方应该是军事重地才对，你们是怎么……
t_en: You better not be trying to scam me. He's working in a top secret military area. How did you manage to...

t_ch: 等等…这的确是他的笔迹，肯定不会错。
t_en: Hold on... This is his handwriting, alright. I'm sure of it.

t_ch: 哎，老爸他啊…还是和以前一样喜欢跟自己较真呢。
t_en: *sigh* My father... he hasn't changed. Always so hard on himself.

t_ch: 那个，抱歉，这个请求听着可能有点突然——如果你有时间的话，能不能去帮我买一束花？
t_en: Uh, I'm sorry to make such a sudden request, but... if you have time, could you buy me a bunch of flowers?

t_ch: 我想给爸爸写一封回信，和花束放在一起送给他。
t_en: I want to write him a letter and send it together with a bouquet.

t_ch: 嗨，帮人帮到底吧。
t_en: *sigh* I can't really stop halfway, can I?

t_ch: 举手之劳。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 你答应了？谢谢！
t_en: You will? Thank you so much!

t_ch: 这附近就有一家花店，能麻烦你们帮我带一束「七彩虹」回来吗？当然了，买花和跑腿的钱由我来出。
t_en: There's a florist nearby. Please get me a bunch of rainbows. Naturally, I'll pay you for the flowers and running this errand for me.

t_ch: 那就辛苦你了，一会儿见！
t_en: Thanks again, I'll see you later!

t_ch: 我想买一束「七彩虹」。
t_en: I want to buy a bunch of Rainbows please.

t_ch: 「七彩虹」，对吧？没问题，这就帮你挑几株。
t_en: Rainbows, right? No problem, I'll pick some out for you now.

t_ch: 请问，这些花是打算送给谁的？
t_en: May I ask who these flowers are for?

t_ch: 呃…长辈？
t_en: Uh... a parent?

t_ch: 哦？送「七彩虹」给长辈，还真是…另类的选择。
t_en: Oh? Rainbows for a parent is... quite a unique choice.

t_ch: 你知道吗？「七彩虹」的花语是「平凡的美好」，在多年的朋友或者老夫老妻之间比较流行。
t_en: You know, in the language of flowers, rainbows mean "ordinary beauty." They're a popular choice among old friends and couples.

t_ch: 是想让长辈多多享受人生，不要被生活或是工作上的纷繁琐事困扰吗……
t_en: Are you trying to tell your parent to enjoy themselves? To not be troubled by the pettiness of work and life...?

t_ch: 这么一想，好像寓意也不错呢。
t_en: That would be a great message actually.

t_ch: 给你，一束「七彩虹」。欢迎下次光临！
t_en: Here you are, a bunch of rainbows. Hope to see you again soon!

t_ch: 「七彩虹」买来了。
t_en: Here we go, I have bought the Rainbow.

t_ch: 动作可真快！谢谢你们，这就行了。正好，我的回信也写好了。等你下次见到老爸的时候，麻烦连着花一起带给他吧。
t_en: You sure are quick! Thank you, that should be perfect. I just finished writing my reply letter as well. Please give it to my father the next time you go see him.

t_ch: 他啊，性格一直都是这么别扭，总是会找理由跟自己过不去。他在信上写的那些事迹肯定不是他的亲身经历，这点我还是能分辨出来的。
t_en: He's always been quite stubborn and too hard on himself. I can tell that the things he wrote about in the letter aren't really what he has done.

t_ch: 从我记事起，老爸就喜欢在我面前逞英雄、说些不着边际的大话…他大概是觉得，如果自己在部队里没有获得什么名头，家人的脸上也会没光吧？
t_en: Ever since I can remember, he always liked to act like a hero in front of me and exaggerate things... He probably thinks that if he doesn't achieve something big in the Silvermane Guards, he will be a shame to the family.

t_ch: 但我可从来没那么想过。他努力做好自己的工作，让我过上了比一般人都好的生活…光是这一点，我就已经非常感激了。
t_en: But I've never cared about that. He's always worked hard to give me a better life, better than most... That's already enough for me to be truly grateful for him.

t_ch: 哎呀，不好意思，一不注意就跟你说了一大堆家务事。反正…我只希望老爸能平平安安地回到家里就行了。
t_en: Argh, sorry for going on about our family matters. Anyway... I just hope that my father can return home safely.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你回来了，真快啊！不愧是年轻人，手脚就是灵便。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're back so soon! Young people these days really are quick on their feet.

t_ch: 你们见着莉拉了吗？把我的信转交给她了没？她说什么了吗？
t_en: Did you go see Lila? And did you give her my letter? What did she say?

t_ch: 她给你写了一封回信。
t_en: She wrote a letter for you in reply.

t_ch: 别着急，你看…
t_en: Relax, here, take a look...

t_ch: 她、她给我回信了？快，拿给我看看……
t_en: Sh—She wrote back to me? Quick, let me read it...

t_ch: 莉拉……
t_en: Lila...

t_ch: 我真是个死脑筋，居然一直在逃避自己的家人，还编出那些没谱的谎话……
t_en: I've been so stubborn... avoiding my own family, making up such ridiculous lies...

t_ch: 我现在明白了，哪怕我这二十多年都没干出什么丰功伟绩，家人也会一直陪在我身边。我之前焦虑的那些事…啧，想想都觉得好笑，哈哈哈。
t_en: I get it now... Even if I don't have any great achievements over these past twenty-plus years, my family will always be by my side. What I was worrying over... Tsk, it's funny to think about now, haha.

t_ch: 谢谢你们帮我这么多忙，陌生人！请一定收下这些…现在我该去好好收拾收拾东西，准备回家了。
t_en: Thank you for all your help, stranger! Please take these... Now I need to go get my things together and get ready to return home.

t_ch: 今天需要帮忙吗，老头子？
t_en: Do you need help, old man?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，年轻人，你来的真是时候！来来来，我正好有个大新闻要跟你分享……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, young one, you arrive just in time! Come here, I have big news to share with you...

t_ch: 呃…也不知道该算好消息还是坏消息？后勤部的一帮小年轻听说我要退伍了，全都跑来挽留…他们说，要是没有我这个老兵带着，他们肯定办不成事。
t_en: Though I'm not sure it can be considered as good news... Youngsters from the Logistics Department were trying to convince me to stay after they heard that I was retiring... They said that they couldn't handle things correctly without me leading the way.

t_ch: 他们可能只是礼貌一下…
t_en: Maybe they were just being polite...

t_ch: …你当真这么觉得？可是他们已经把这事报告给上面了……
t_en: You really think so? But they've even asked their superior about this...

t_ch: 得让年轻人学会自己成长。
t_en: Young people need to learn and grow by themselves.

t_ch: …我一开始的想法和你一样。不过我没想到，他们真的把这事报告给上面了……
t_en: ...I thought the same at the beginning. But I wasn't expecting that they even asked their superior about this...

t_ch: 邓恩那小子之前跑来跟我谈话，问我愿不愿意再多待几个月，培养培养接班人。
t_en: Then Dunn came to me and asked if I wanted to stay for another couple of months to train my successors.

t_ch: …哎呀，你也知道我脸皮薄，哪能拒绝这么真情实意的邀请！
t_en: ...Ah, you know me, too shy to turn down such a sincere request!

t_ch: 你就这么答应了？
t_en: So you've said yes?

t_ch: 那莉拉怎么办？
t_en: Then how about Lila?

t_ch: 我当时心情比较激动，就一口答应下来了。
t_en: I was a bit emotional at that point and agreed to his request.

t_ch: 但事后仔细一想，感觉真是对不起老婆孩子…这下她们又得再多等几个月了。
t_en: But when I gave it a second thought later, I felt bad for my wife and child... They have to wait for another handful of months.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，年轻人——你愿意再帮我一次吗？我写了封信向母女俩解释这件事，要是你能替我捎给莉拉那就再好不过了……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, young one, are you willing to help me once again? I wrote a letter to my wife and child to explain my situation. If you could deliver my letter to Lila...

t_ch: 举手之劳。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 多谢你！真希望后勤部的年轻人都能跟你一样靠谱。
t_en: Many thanks! Hope those youngsters in the Logistics Department can be as reliable as you are.

t_ch: 你确定不会后悔？
t_en: Are you sure you won't regret it?

t_ch: 嗨，有什么好后悔的…能在我熟悉的岗位上再多发光发热一阵，也好……
t_en: Nothing to be regretful for... It would only be worth it to shine in my field for a while longer...

t_ch: 哦，对了——你能顺便去花店买束花，和信一起转交给莉拉吗？她之前送了我一束花，我也想回赠一次……
t_en: While you are at it, can you go pick some flowers at the Florist for Lila? Last time, she gave me a bouquet. I want to give her something in return...

t_ch: 至于选什么品种的花…就由你定吧。我现在也不太清楚她的喜好……
t_en: As for the best type of flowers... I'll let you decide. I'm not so sure about her favorites now...

t_ch: 莉拉…原谅老爸。不过几个月的时间，一眨眼就会过去的……
t_en: Lila... Forgive dad. It'll just be several months. It won't be long...

t_ch: 我想买一束花当作礼物…
t_en: I'd like to buy some flowers as a present.

t_ch: 送礼？当然没问题！
t_en: Then you came to the right place!

t_ch: 请问您打算给什么人送花呢？不妨告诉我，说不定我能帮您出出点子。
t_en: If you could tell me more about the person you're sending flowers to, I can give you some suggestions.

t_ch: 替一位父亲送给他久别的女儿。
t_en: The flowers are a gift to a daughter from her long-absent father.

t_ch: 原来如此，是要送给许久未见的亲人啊……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 那我推荐您买上一束「球牡丹」——这种花一年只会盛开一次，每次开花的时间都不超过一个月。
t_en: Then I recommend the Ball Peonies — they only bloom once a year, and each bloom lasts for less than a month.

t_ch: 「球牡丹」的花语是「耐心」…相信只要耐心等待，家人之间终会团圆。
t_en: In the language of flowers, Ball Peony means "patience." It takes a bit of patience, but the family will eventually be reunited.

t_ch: 就来一束「球牡丹」吧。
t_en: Then I'll take a bouquet of Ball Peonies.

t_ch: 当然！我这就帮您挑上几株……
t_en: Sure! One moment, please.

t_ch: 都准备好了。请拿好您的「球牡丹」——欢迎下次再光临「长夏」花店。
t_en: Here are your Ball Peonies. Thanks for the purchase, and have a lovely day!

t_ch: 送给一位年轻美丽的女士。
t_en: The flowers will be given to a beautiful girl.

t_ch: 原来如此，是要送给一位年轻的女士啊……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 那我推荐您买上一束「七彩虹」——这种小花色彩斑斓艳丽，是年轻人之间送礼的不二之选。
t_en: Then I recommend the Rainbows — these small flowers are a riot of color, perfect for gift giving between young people.

t_ch: 「七彩虹」的花语是「平凡的美好」。活在这个年代，还有什么比「平凡的美好」更值得珍惜的呢？
t_en: In the language of flowers, Rainbow means "simple beauty." In times like this, what else is more precious than "simple beauty?"

t_ch: 就来一束「七彩虹」吧。
t_en: Then I'll take a bouquet of Rainbows.

t_ch: 当然！我这就帮您挑上几株……
t_en: Sure! One moment, please.

t_ch: 都准备好了。请拿好您的「七彩虹」——欢迎下次再光临「长夏」花店。
t_en: Here are your Rainbows. Thanks for the purchase, and have a lovely day!

t_ch: 送给一位热爱动物的爱心人士。
t_en: The flowers will be given to a kind animal lover.

t_ch: 说起来，我已经很久没在贝洛伯格见过动物了…您要送花的对象如此喜欢动物，看来是位相当善良体贴的人呢。
t_en: Speaking of animals, I haven't seen any in Belobog in a long time... The person you are sending flowers to must be a very kind person.

t_ch: 那我推荐您买上一束「初雪八落」——这种花的花语就是「纯洁善良」。我猜，它和您送花的对象一定非常搭配。
t_en: Then I recommend the First Snows — they stand for "pure and kind-hearted" in the language of flowers. I think they'll suit the recipient of your present very well.

t_ch: 就来一束「初雪八落」吧。
t_en: Then I'll take a bouquet of First Snows.

t_ch: 当然！我这就帮您挑上几株……
t_en: Sure! One moment, please.

t_ch: 都准备好了。请拿好您的「初雪八落」——欢迎下次再光临「长夏」花店。
t_en: Here are your First Snows. Thanks for the purchase, and have a lovely day!

t_ch: 给我你最名贵的花。
t_en: I'll take a bouquet of the most precious flowers here.

t_ch: 最名贵的花…您要送花的对象，她的身份肯定很尊贵吧？
t_en: The most precious... I assume you're sending flowers to a person of very high status?

t_ch: 那我推荐您买上一株「侯爵夫人」——这是贝洛伯格最尊贵、最美丽、也是最难培育的一种花。
t_en: Then my recommendation would be the Marquises. They are the most exalted, beautiful, and delicate flowers in Belobog.

t_ch: 凡是贝洛伯格的上流人士，一定都拒绝不了「侯爵夫人」的艳丽。只是…这种花的价格不菲，不知道您意下如何？
t_en: No one in high society would turn down a bouquet of Marquises. It's just that... Marquises are quite expensive. Are you sure you want to buy some?

t_ch: 就来一株「侯爵夫人」吧。
t_en: I'll take a bouquet of Marquises.

t_ch: 当然！我这就帮您挑上一株……
t_en: Sure! One moment, please.

t_ch: 都准备好了。请拿好您的「侯爵夫人」——欢迎下次再光临「长夏」花店。
t_en: Here are your Marquises. Thanks for the purchase, and have a lovely day!

t_ch: 我带来了你爸爸的信。
t_en: Your dad wrote you a letter. Here.

t_ch: 老爸又来信了？他最近可真恋家。多谢你帮他捎信——可不能让你白跑一趟，我得准备些谢礼才行！
t_en: Another letter? He seems pretty homesick lately. Thanks for bringing it to me. Please, let me give you something for your trouble.

t_ch: 这是…球牡丹？是老爸拜托你给我买的吗？
t_en: These are... Ball Peonies? Did my dad ask you to buy these for me?

t_ch: 真是可爱的小花…没想到老爸还有这份心，妈妈看到了也会很开心的。
t_en: Aww... I didn't expect him to be this sensitive. Mom would be very happy too when she sees these pretty flowers.

t_ch: 这是…七彩虹？是老爸拜托你给我买的吗？
t_en: These are... Rainbows? Did my dad ask you to buy these for me?

t_ch: 这算是之的回礼吗？没想到老爸他这么细心…妈妈知道了肯定也会很开心的。
t_en: This must be a return present. I didn't expect Dad to be this thoughtful... Mom would be very happy too when she sees these pretty flowers.

t_ch: 这是…初雪八落？是老爸拜托你给我买的吗？
t_en: These are... First Snows? Did my dad ask you to buy these for me?

t_ch: 真是可爱的小花…没想到老爸还有这份心，妈妈看到了也会很开心的。
t_en: Aww... I didn't expect him to be this sensitive. Mom would be very happy too when she sees these pretty flowers.

t_ch: 哇！这是…侯爵夫人？！这么名贵的花，是老爸拜托你给我买的吗？
t_en: Whoa! These are... Marquises? Did my dad ask you to buy these for me?

t_ch: 这花…真美，不愧是贝洛伯格最名贵的花。可惜妈妈她好像对这种花过敏……
t_en: They're gorgeous... Now I understand why they say Marquises are the most precious flowers in Belobog. It's a pity that Mom's allergic to it...

t_ch: 啊，光顾着看花，都忘了还有老爸的信了。让我看看……
t_en: Uh, I almost forgot about the letter. Lemme see...

t_ch: 莉拉读完了吉尔伯特的信……
t_en: Lila finished the letter from Gilbert...

t_ch: 也就是说，老爸他还要过几个月才能回家……
t_en: So Dad won't be home for a few more months...

t_ch: 他脸皮比较薄。
t_en: He's just too nice to say no.

t_ch: 嗐，他向来都是这样……
t_en: I know... He's always like that.

t_ch: 你生气了？
t_en: Are you upset?

t_ch: 嗐，我怎么会生他的气呢……
t_en: Of course not...

t_ch: 其实，我从这字里行间能感觉出来，老爸他对自己在做的事明显还残留着热情…只是他不太好意思承认罢了。
t_en: Actually, reading between the lines, I can see that Dad's still passionate about his job. He just didn't want to admit it.

t_ch: 其实他不用一直在信里跟我们道歉…只要他还能在自己的岗位上找到认同感，我和妈妈肯定支持他的选择。
t_en: He really doesn't need to apologize to Mom and me. We'd support his choice if the job makes him feel needed and fulfilled.

t_ch: 不过，他这把年纪，想做出什么彻头彻尾的改变恐怕很难吧？所以，我们还是在他身后默默支持就好了，不用在沟通的时候掺进多余的说教。
t_en: Though, at his age, it'd be very hard to change who he is. So, we'll just support him without unnecessary persuasion.

t_ch: 即使相隔两地，也要彼此理解、互相体谅——这才是家人之间的相处方式呀。
t_en: Be understanding and considerate to each other even when separated. This is the meaning of family.

t_ch: 谢谢你帮老爸捎信，{NICKNAME}。这是之前答应好给你的谢礼，请一定收下。
t_en: Thank you for bringing my dad's letter, {NICKNAME}. Please accept these for your trouble.

t_ch: 路过而已，您请继续…
t_en: Just passing by. Please continue...

t_ch: 我们习惯了将「时间」比喻成流动的物体，将「空间」理解为不动的舞台…也许，一切是相反的…也许，流动的是「空间」，而「时间」则是可以被穿凿的磐石……
t_en: We're used to thinking of "time" as something that flows, and "space" as something static... But what if everything was the other way around? Perhaps it's "space" that moves, while "time" is the one that's set in stone...

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: 别出声！哦，你没出声…那你也不要突然出现！给我思路都打断了！
t_en: Shhh! Oh, you didn't say anything... Then don't just show up like that and break my train of thought!

t_ch: 你在表演话剧吗？
t_en: Are you acting in a play?

t_ch: 别出声！你管这叫话剧？这是科研！给我思路都打断了！
t_en: Shhhh! Do you think this is theater? This is research! Don't break my train of thought!

t_ch: 我方才分明看见宇宙盛大的图景在我眼前喷薄而出，即将把她伟大的智慧倾泻在我同样伟大的头脑中，却被你打断了！
t_en: I'd just caught sight of the magnificence of the universe gushing towards me, about to pour out her great wisdom into my equally great mind when you interrupted me!

t_ch: 还说你没在演话剧…
t_en: Aren't you being a bit too dramatic?

t_ch: 你的修辞好诡异…
t_en: You're being really weird.

t_ch: 哎，你真是让我深刻理解了什么叫「夏虫不可语冰」。
t_en: And you're being really unenlightened.

t_ch: 仔细一看，你不是那个喜欢多管闲事的人吗？那你来得正好，有些事情需要你帮忙。
t_en: Wait a minute, aren't you that meddler who loves to get involved in other people's business? Well, you're in luck, as I do need a helper right now.

t_ch: 危险吗？我还不想为科学献身…
t_en: Is it dangerous? I'm not yet ready to sacrifice for science.

t_ch: 呵呵，你有这个资格吗？别担心，不是什么大事。
t_en: Humph. Are you even qualified to make any sacrifice? Anyway, don't worry about it. It's no big deal at all.

t_ch: 呃，不了吧。
t_en: Er, no thanks.

t_ch: 别担心，不是什么大事。
t_en: Don't worry about it. It's no big deal at all.

t_ch: 况且，你刚刚打断了我的思路。往小了说，这是断送了又一个伟大理论的诞生，往大了说，你就是断送了时空科学的未来啊！难道你不该赎罪吗？
t_en: Besides, it was you who interrupted my train of thought, potentially ruining the birth of a groundbreaking scientific theory or even jeopardizing the very future of space science itself! Don't you think it's only fair that you make amends by helping me now?

t_ch: 给你这个，这是我关于「大宇宙时间隧道」这一科学发现的论文。这篇划时代的论文本该被大守护者读到，应用于科研建设，为万世开太平。
t_en: Check out this paper of mine on the theory about the big universe time tunnel. This groundbreaking research should have been studied by the Supreme Guardian and applied to the research and development of our world.

t_ch: 可那几个尸位素餐的官僚，一直在想方设法刁难我！
t_en: But those pencil-pushing bureaucrats are always trying to make trouble for me!

t_ch: 我只需要你帮我做一件很简单的事：把这篇论文交给大守护者，让她看到我的成果。
t_en: I just need your help with this very simple thing: Give this thesis to the Supreme Guardian. Let her see what I've achieved.

t_ch: 你自己怎么不去？
t_en: Why don't you go yourself?

t_ch: 我不去，那帮官僚都认识我了。我现在跑过去还不够受羞辱的呢！哎，羞辱科学家，是官僚系统朽坏的直接征兆，可恶！可悲！
t_en: Those cronies would do nothing but make fun of me. It's all because of their corruption that I'm in this debacle to begin with. How shameful!

t_ch: 太简单了，这就出发。
t_en: Simple enough. Let's go.

t_ch: 好！看来虽然你缺乏科学素养、盲目迷信传统科学、缺乏独立思考能力、肚里没有半滴墨，但人还是不错的。最起码比那帮官僚强多了。
t_en: Great! Despite your lack of scientific acumen, your blind belief in the superstitions of traditional science, your lack of critical thinking skills, and your limited cultural prowess, you still seem to be a decent person. Much better than those darn bureaucrats, at least.

t_ch: 有点难度，得加钱。
t_en: It's tough. Gotta spend more.

t_ch: 好说，只要你能办妥…我高低得给你整幢小别墅。那时候我估计已经是知名科学家了，不会亏待你的。
t_en: Well, as long as you can get it done... I might even buy a whole house for you. I bet I would be famous then, and I'll sure make it worth your while.
